task_completion.description: Verify the three required buses ('Drum Bus', 'Music Bus', 'Vocal Bus') have been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for new channel strips or track headers labeled 'Drum Bus', 'Music Bus', and 'Vocal Bus' in the Ardour editor or mixer window.

task_completion.description: Verify the Kick, Snare, and HiHat tracks are routed to the 'Drum Bus' and not directly to Master.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the mixer view, the output routing section (usually at the bottom or top of the channel strip) for Kick, Snare, and HiHat should display 'Drum Bus' instead of 'Master'.

task_completion.description: Verify the Bass and Guitar tracks are routed to the 'Music Bus', and the Vocals track is routed to the 'Vocal Bus' (and not directly to Master).
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the mixer view, the output routing section for Bass and Guitar should display 'Music Bus', and for Vocals it should display 'Vocal Bus' instead of 'Master'.

task_completion.description: Verify the three newly created buses are routed to the Master bus.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The output routing section for the 'Drum Bus', 'Music Bus', and 'Vocal Bus' channel strips should display 'Master'.

task_completion.description: Verify the fader gains for the buses are set to the specified values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the numerical gain readouts or fader positions on the bus channel strips. 'Drum Bus' should be near -3.0 dB, 'Music Bus' near -2.0 dB, and 'Vocal Bus' near 0.0 dB (within a 1.5 dB tolerance).

task_completion.description: Verify the Ardour session was saved after making the changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the agent clicking 'File' -> 'Save', using the Ctrl+S shortcut, or the absence of an unsaved indicator (like an asterisk '*') next to the session name in the window title bar at the end of the trajectory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the Ardour GUI to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Ardour application open with menus, mixer, or routing grid being actively used, rather than terminal commands or text editors modifying the session XML file directly.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply type the expected answers into a text file to spoof completion.
integrity.visual_evidence: The routing and gain changes must be visible within the Ardour interface itself, not just written in a text document.

integrity.description: Verify the routing was done using Ardour's routing matrix or mixer strip outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the routing grid window or the output dropdown menus on the mixer strips being clicked and modified.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Buses: 'Drum Bus', 'Music Bus', 'Vocal Bus'. Routing Map: Kick, Snare, HiHat -> Drum Bus; Bass, Guitar -> Music Bus; Vocals -> Vocal Bus. Expected Gains: Drum Bus = -3.0 dB, Music Bus = -2.0 dB, Vocal Bus = 0.0 dB (Tolerance: +/- 1.5 dB).
